subject: Statistics
title: Overfitting in Forecasting
description: A 60-second lesson on Overfitting in Forecasting.

hook.text: A model fits past data perfectly — every twist and turn. Then it forecasts terribly. It memorized noise.

buildup.text: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex for the data. It captures random variation as if it were a pattern.

discovery.text: Train on old data, test on recent data. Good on both means generalizing. Good on old only means overfitting.

twist.text: Adding more parameters always improves the fit to training data. But it can destroy forecast accuracy.

climax.text: AIC and BIC penalize complexity. They balance fit against the number of parameters to prevent overfitting.

punchline.text: A perfect fit to the past is a warning. Simplicity forecasts better.

quiz.question: How do AIC and BIC help prevent overfitting?
options: They penalize model complexity — balancing good fit against using too many parameters
They remove outliers from the data
They automatically select the simplest possible model
